# fix(nexus): rename PermissionError→ForbiddenError + batch-tag-push rule

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `c3fbe374b24d` — fix(nexus): rename PermissionError→ForbiddenError + batch-tag-push rule

**Body**:

Resolves the three outstanding follow-ups from the 2026-04-14 release.

## Security M1: PermissionError shadows stdlib — renamed to ForbiddenError

The `nexus.errors.PermissionError` class shadowed Python's stdlib
`PermissionError` exception. Any consumer doing `from nexus.errors import *`
or `except PermissionError:` in unrelated code would silently catch a
403 HTTP error instead of an OS permission denial.

Fix: renamed the class to `ForbiddenError` (the canonical name matching
the HTTP status). Kept `PermissionError = ForbiddenError` as a deprecated
module-level alias so existing imports continue to work. Internal
`core.py` call sites migrated to `ForbiddenError`. Public
`NexusPermissionError` alias retained on `nexus/__init__.py` for
backwards compatibility with external consumers.

Runtime behavior is unchanged (same class under both names). Any code
using `except nexus.NexusPermissionError` or `except NexusError` still
catches the error identically.

Bumps kailash-nexus 2.0.2 → 2.0.3 (patch: API addition + deprecated
alias, no breaking change).

## Batch tag push rule: push release tags individually

On 2026-04-14, `git push origin v2.8.6 dataflow-v2.0.8 kaizen-v2.7.4
nexus-v2.0.2 mcp-v0.2.4` triggered ZERO workflow runs. All 5 packages
had to be published via manual `workflow_dispatch`. Root cause: GitHub
Actions' `push.tags` webhook has undocumented rate-limiting when
multiple tags arrive in a single push event.

Added MUST rule to rules/deployment.md requiring individual tag pushes
with ≥1s pause, and documented the failure mode + recovery procedure
in deploy/deployment-config.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
